definition.name: exposed_face
definition.definition: A face F of a convex set K is called exposed if there exists a continuous affine functional h : K → R and a value β such that F = {x ∈ K : h(x) = β} = K ∩ {h = β}, where h(y) ≥ β for all y ∈ K (i.e., F is cut out by a supporting hyperplane). Every exposed face is a face, but not every face is exposed. The face generated by a point F_K(x) may fail to be exposed. The distinction matters because Pataki's formula applies to faces of cones defined by affine equalities (which are exposed), while the Weis-Shirokov theorem applies to faces generated by points (which are the correct notion for Barvinok-Pataki bounds). For state spaces of C*-algebras, exposed faces correspond to faces of the form {φ : φ(a) = 0} for positive a ∈ A, which are in bijection with closed left ideals. References: Alfsen, Compact Convex Sets and Boundary Integrals, §I.2; Weis, arXiv:2404.00832.
